member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.DevicesInformation
member.summary: A class to get the HyperWire devices that are connected to an Automation1 controller. Access an instance of this class through a object instance.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.DevicesInformation.#ctor(Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.RefreshableLazy{Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCard},Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.RefreshableLazy{System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireDevice}})
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.DevicesInformation.HyperWireCard
member.summary: Gets the active PCIe HyperWire card used by a PC-Based Automation1 controller to communicate with connected HyperWire devices (), or null if a PCIe HyperWire card is not in use.
member.remarks: There might be more than one PCIe HyperWire card installed in the PC but this property is the single PCIe HyperWire card currently being used by the Automation1 controller. This property will be null if the Automation1 controller is operating without a HyperWire card (i.e. in virtual mode or if it is a drive-based controller).

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.DevicesInformation.HyperWireDevices
member.summary: Gets the collection of HyperWire devices connected to the Automation1 controller over the HyperWire motion bus.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCard
member.summary: Represents a PCIe HyperWire card used by a PC-Based Automation1 controller to communicate with connected HyperWire devices.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCard.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Version)
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCard.BusNumber
member.summary: Gets the PCI bus number this PCIe HyperWire card is on. This is used along with and to uniquely identify the PCIe card in the PC.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCard.DeviceNumber
member.summary: Gets the PCI device number for this PCIe HyperWire card. This is used along with and to uniquely identify the PCIe card in the PC.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCard.FunctionNumber
member.summary: Gets the PCI function number for this PCIe HyperWire card. This is used along with and to uniquely identify the PCIe card in the PC.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCard.VendorId
member.summary: Gets the vendor ID of this PCIe HyperWire card.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCard.DeviceId
member.summary: Gets the device ID of this PCIe HyperWire card.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCard.FirmwareVersion
member.summary: Gets the version of firmware running on this PCIe HyperWire card.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCardPort
member.summary: Represents one of the communication ports on a PCIe HyperWire card.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCardPort.None
member.summary: No port on the HyperWire card or not connected to a HyperWire card.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCardPort.PortA
member.summary: The 'A' port on a HyperWire card.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireCardPort.PortB
member.summary: The 'B' port on a HyperWire card.

member.summary: Gets the type of device this object represents.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireDevice.FirmwareVersion
member.summary: Gets the version of firmware running on this HyperWire device.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireDevice.DeviceIndex
member.summary: Gets the numeric index of this HyperWire device as it is connected to the Automation1 controller over the HyperWire motion bus.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireDevice.BackplaneSlot
member.summary: Gets the backplane slot of this HyperWire device.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireDevice.HyperWireCardPort
member.summary: Gets which communication port this HyperWire device is connected to on a PCIe HyperWire card, if any.
member.remarks: This will be if the HyperWire device is not connected to a HyperWire card (i.e. if it is a drive-based controller).

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.HyperWireDevice.Axes
member.summary: Gets the collection of axes that this HyperWire device has, if any.
member.remarks: If this HyperWire device has no axes then this collection will be empty. HyperWire devices with axes are known as drives. This collection is not indexed by axis index or by axis name, instead it is simply an array-like collection that always starts at index 0.

member.summary: Represents a motion target mode.

member.summary: Motion target positions are relative to the current axis locations.

member.summary: Motion target positions are absolute.

member.summary: Represents a motion waiting mode.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.WaitMode.MotionDone
member.summary: Wait for the motion to be done before continuing execution.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.WaitMode.InPosition
member.summary: Wait for the motion to be done and for the position error to reach a specified value before continuing execution.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.WaitMode.Auto
member.summary: Wait the minimum quantity of time between motion blocks before continuing execution.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.NormalcyAlignment
member.summary: Represents the type of normalcy alignment.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.NormalcyAlignment.Left
member.summary: Keeps the normalcy axis perpendicular and to the left of the part.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.NormalcyAlignment.Right
member.summary: Keeps the normalcy axis perpendicular and to the right of the part.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.NormalcyAlignment.Relative
member.summary: Keeps the normalcy axis at a relative angle to the part.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ReturnType.Interrupt
member.summary: The axes return to the positions where the interruption occurred.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ReturnType.Offset
member.summary: The axes do not return to the positions where the interruption occurred, and subsequent moves have an offset added.

member.summary: Opens a new file and overwrites the existing file.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.FileMode.Read
member.summary: Opens an existing file only for reading.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.FileMode.Append
member.summary: Opens an existing file only for appending data to the end of the file.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TimerMode.Standard
member.summary: Retrieves the timer value as an integer number of milliseconds.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TimerMode.Precise
member.summary: Retrieves the timer value with a resolution of 0.0001 milliseconds.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.DateTimeStringFormat
member.summary: Represents the format to use when creating a date and time string.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.DateTimeStringFormat.Default
member.summary: Create the date and time string using this format "[DayOfWeek] YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS [TimeZone]".

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.DateTimeStringFormat.Iso8601Local
member.summary: Create the date and time string using the ISO-8601 Local format, which looks like "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS".

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.DateTimeStringFormat.Iso8601Utc
member.summary: Create the date and time string using the ISO-8601 UTC format, which looks like "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ".

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.DateTimeStringFormat.Iso8601UtcWithOffset
member.summary: Create the date and time string using the ISO-8601 UTC format with the UTC offset, which looks like "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS+HHMM" or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS-HHMM".

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.IndicatorAnimationType
member.summary: Represents the animation type of an App Indicator.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.IndicatorAnimationType.Solid
member.summary: Solid background using $backgroundColor1 and $foregroundColor1.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.IndicatorAnimationType.Flash
member.summary: Flashes between $backgroundColor1 and transparent with no transition colors between the two. The foreground color is $foregroundColor1 for the full animation.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.IndicatorAnimationType.TwoColorFlash
member.summary: Flashes between $backgroundColor1 and $backgroundColor2 with no transition colors between the two. Also alternates the $displayText between $foregroundColor1 and $foregroundColor2.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.IndicatorAnimationType.ColorSweep
member.summary: Transitions smoothly between $backgroundColor1 and $backgroundColor2 with intermediary colors. Also transitions the $displayText between $foregroundColor1 and $foregroundColor2.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoLaser
member.summary: Represents the mode for controlling a laser.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoLaser.Off
member.summary: Manually turns off the laser.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoLaser.On
member.summary: Manually turns on the laser.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoLaser.Auto
member.summary: Specifies that the laser is automatically controlled.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoWobbleMode
member.summary: Specifies the mode of the wobble pattern.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoWobbleMode.TimeBased
member.summary: The wobble is repeated at a fixed time interval.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoWobbleMode.DistanceBased
member.summary: The wobble is repeated at a fixed vector distance.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoWobbleType
member.summary: Specifies the type of the wobble pattern.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoWobbleType.Ellipse
member.summary: The pattern uses an ellipse shape.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoWobbleType.Figure8Parallel
member.summary: The pattern uses a figure 8 shape that is parallel to the vector path.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.GalvoWobbleType.Figure8Perpendicular
member.summary: The pattern uses a figure 8 shape that is perpendicular to the vector path.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoDistanceCounterResetMask
member.summary: Specifies the conditions that will reset the PSO distance counters.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoDistanceCounterResetMask.ResetUntilMarker
member.summary: The counters will be held in reset until the first marker is encountered.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoDistanceCounterResetMask.ResetOnMarker
member.summary: The counters will reset when the marker is encountered.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoDistanceCounterResetMask.ResetOutsideWindow
member.summary: The counters will be held in reset when the PSO window output is off.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoDistanceCounterResetMask.ResetWhenLaserOff
member.summary: The counters will be held in reset when the laser command bit is off.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoDistanceCounterResetMask.ResetWhenOutputOff
member.summary: The counters will be held in reset when the PSO output is off.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoDistanceAllowedEventDirection
member.summary: Specifies the motion directions that can generate PSO events.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoDistanceAllowedEventDirection.Both
member.summary: PSO events will be generated when the distance counter reaches the configured distance in both directions.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoDistanceAllowedEventDirection.Positive
member.summary: PSO events will only be generated when the distance counter reaches the configured distance in the positive direction.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoDistanceAllowedEventDirection.Negative
member.summary: PSO events will only be generated when the distance counter reaches the configured distance in the negative direction.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowUpdateDirection
member.summary: Specifies the directions in which exiting the active PSO window will result in the window updating to the next pair of ranges.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowUpdateDirection.Both
member.summary: The PSO window range will update when exiting the active window in either direction.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowUpdateDirection.Positive
member.summary: The PSO window range will only update when exiting the active window in the positive direction.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowUpdateDirection.Negative
member.summary: The PSO window range will only update when exiting the active window in the negative direction.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowEventDirection
member.summary: Specifies the directions in which internal window events will be generated upon entering or exiting the window.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowEventDirection.Both
member.summary: An internal window event will be generated when entering or exiting the active window in either direction.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowEventDirection.Positive
member.summary: An internal window event will be generated when entering or exiting the active window in the positive direction.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowEventDirection.Negative
member.summary: An internal window event will be generated when entering or exiting the active window in the negative direction.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowCounterResetMask
member.summary: Specifies the conditions that will reset the PSO window counters.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowCounterResetMask.ResetUntilMarker
member.summary: The counters will be held in reset until the first marker is encountered.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowCounterResetMask.ResetOnMarker
member.summary: The counters will reset when the marker is encountered.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowEventMode
member.summary: Specifies the conditions that will generate a PSO event when entering or exiting the PSO window.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowEventMode.None
member.summary: An event will not be generated when entering or exiting the window.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowEventMode.Enter
member.summary: An event will be generated when entering the window.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowEventMode.Exit
member.summary: An event will be generated when exiting the window.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWindowEventMode.Both
member.summary: An event will be generated when entering or exiting the window.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoEventMask
member.summary: Specifies additional conditions to prevent PSO events from occurring.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoEventMask.WindowMask
member.summary: PSO events will not occur when the window output is off.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoEventMask.WindowMaskInvert
member.summary: PSO events will not occur when the window output is on.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoEventMask.LaserMask
member.summary: PSO events will not occur when the laser command bit is off.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoEventMask.BitMask
member.summary: PSO events will not occur when the active PSO bit is 0.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWaveformMode
member.summary: Selects the output mode of the PSO waveform module.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoWaveformMode.Pulse

member.summary: Wrapping mode for the camming table.

member.summary: Table leader axis values will not wrap. This is the default controller behavior and is correct for most applications.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.CammingWrapping.Wrap
member.summary: Table leader axis values wrap creating a cyclic array. This is useful for rotary applications where the first position is 0 degrees and the final position is 360 degrees.

member.summary: Specifies the input data source for camming motion.

member.summary: Use Position Feedback on the leader axis as the source for camming.

member.summary: Use Position Command on the leader axis as the source for camming.

member.summary: Use Auxiliary Feedback on the leader axis as the source for camming.

member.summary: Use Sync Port A as the source for camming.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.CammingSource.SyncPortB
member.summary: Use Sync Port B as the source for camming.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.CammingOutput
member.summary: Specifies the output signal to generate on the camming follower axis. Also specifies the leader axis and follower axis synchronization to use when camming is enabled.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.CammingOutput.RelativePosition
member.summary: The follower axis will operate in position-based relative synchronization mode. All camming table values for the follower axis are treated as absolute positions, but the follower axis will remain at the current position when camming is enabled. An offset will be applied to all follower axis positions in the camming table, and the offset is computed based on the current leader axis position and current follower axis position.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.CammingOutput.AbsolutePosition
member.summary: The follower axis will operate in position-based absolute synchronization mode. All camming table values for the follower axis are treated as absolute positions, and the controller will perform an alignment move on the follower axis when camming is enabled. The controller moves the follower axis to the position specified in the camming table based on the current leader axis position.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.CammingOutput.Velocity
member.summary: The follower axis will operate in velocity mode. All camming table values for the follower axis are treated as velocities, but the follower axis will remain at the current velocity when camming is enabled. An offset will be applied to all follower axis velocities in the camming table, and the offset is computed based on the current leader axis position and current follower axis velocity.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutofocusFocusMode
member.summary: Selects whether autofocus will run in continuous focus mode or single focus mode.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutofocusFocusMode.Continuous
member.summary: The autofocus routine runs continuously until you turn off autofocus.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutofocusFocusMode.Single
member.summary: The autofocus routine automatically turns off when the input that is specified by the AutofocusInput parameter is within the specified deadband of the AutofocusDeadband parameter.

member.summary: The analog output will track the Velocity Command item in user units per second.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AnalogOutputAxisTrackingItem.AccelerationCommand
member.summary: The analog output will track the Acceleration Command item in user units per second squared.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AnalogOutputAxisTrackingItem.PositionError
member.summary: The analog output will track the Position Error item in user units.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AnalogOutputAxisTrackingItem.VectorSpeedCommand
member.summary: The analog output will track the vector speed in user units per second.

member.summary: Represents a 1-bit boolean Modbus register value.

member.summary: Represents a 32-bit floating-point Modbus register value.

member.summary: Represents a 16-bit unsigned integer Modbus register value.

member.summary: Represents a 32-bit unsigned integer Modbus register value.

member.summary: Represents an 8-bit signed integer Modbus register value.

member.summary: Represents a 16-bit signed integer Modbus register value.

member.summary: Represents a 32-bit signed integer Modbus register value.

member.summary: The Controller Plus Option is not installed. You cannot use this task.

member.summary: This task was disabled by the EnabledTasks parameter.

member.summary: No program is associated or running.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskState.ProgramReady
member.summary: A program is associated but not running.

member.summary: A program is associated and running.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskState.ProgramFeedhold
member.summary: A program is associated, was run, and feedhold is active. This state is active from the moment the motion begins to decelerate due to a feedhold until the moment the motion begins to accelerate back to speed due to a feedhold release.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskState.ProgramPaused
member.summary: A program is associated, was run, and was paused.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskState.ProgramComplete
member.summary: A program is associated, was run, and completed.(1)

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoStatus.DistanceArrayDepleted
member.summary: The Distance module attempted to load a new array mode distance when no new distance values were available. The distance counters are disabled until new distance values are specified.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoStatus.Window1ArrayDepleted
member.summary: The Window 1 module attempted to load a new array mode window range when no new range values were available. The window output is disabled until new range values are specified.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoStatus.Window2ArrayDepleted
member.summary: The Window 2 module attempted to load a new array mode window range when no new range values were available. The window output is disabled until new range values are specified.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoStatus.BitArrayDepleted
member.summary: The Bit module attempted to load a new bit value when no new bit values were available. The bit output is disabled until new range values are specified.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.PsoStatus.WaveformArrayDepleted
member.summary: The Waveform module attempted to load a new array mode parameter value when no new parameter values were available. The waveform module is disabled until new parameter values are specified.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.StoStatus
member.summary: Represents the Safe Torque Off (STO) status for an axis.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.StoStatus.None
member.summary: No bits set.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.StoStatus.Sto1Activated
member.summary: Safe Torque Off (STO) channel 1 hardware is activated and preventing torque.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.StoStatus.Sto1Event
member.summary: Power has been removed from the Safe Torque Off (STO) channel 1 input. Torque on the motor will be prevented after the hardware time delay.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.StoStatus.Sto2Activated
member.summary: Safe Torque Off (STO) channel 2 hardware is activated and preventing torque.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.StoStatus.Sto2Event
member.summary: Power has been removed from the Safe Torque Off (STO) channel 2 input. Torque on the motor will be prevented after the hardware time delay.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.StoStatus.StoCrossCheckFault
member.summary: The Safe Torque Off (STO) diagnostic check has detected a hardware problem on an STO channel and is preventing torque on the motor.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.BissStatus
member.summary: Represents the BiSS status for a feedback input.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.BissStatus.None
member.summary: No bits set.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.BissStatus.CrcError
member.summary: A CRC error occurred while the drive communicated with the encoder.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.BissStatus.EncoderWarning
member.summary: The warning bit from the encoder is active.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.BissStatus.EncoderError
member.summary: The error bit from the encoder is active.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.BissStatus.TimeoutError
member.summary: The encoder did not respond in the necessary time interval.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.BissStatus.TimeoutError2
member.summary: The encoder did not respond in the necessary time interval.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.None
member.summary: No bits set.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.CalEnabled1D
member.summary: This enum value is deprecated and will be removed in the next major version of Automation1 software. This enum value has been renamed to CalibrationEnabled1D. Use CalibrationEnabled1D instead of this enum value.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.CalEnabled2D
member.summary: This enum value is deprecated and will be removed in the next major version of Automation1 software. This enum value has been renamed to CalibrationEnabled2D. Use CalibrationEnabled2D instead of this enum value.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.Homed
member.summary: The axis is homed.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.Profiling
member.summary: The axis is doing coordinated (MoveLinear(), MoveCw(), MoveCcw()), rapid (MoveRapid()), PVT (MovePvt()), or PT (MovePt()) motion.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.WaitDone
member.summary: The controller finished waiting for motion on this axis to complete. The behavior of this bit depends on the selected wait mode. When in Wait Mode Motion Done, this bit will mimic the Motion Done bit, but when in Wait Mode In Position, this bit will not be active until both the Motion Done bit and the In Position bit are both active.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.CommandValid
member.summary: Motion on the axis is controlled from the SMC.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.Homing
member.summary: The axis is currently homing.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.Enabling
member.summary: The axis is currently enabling.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.MotionRestricted
member.summary: Motion restrictions are currently applied to the axis.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.JogGenerating
member.summary: This bit represents internal status.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.Jogging
member.summary: The axis is performing asynchronous motion (MoveIncremental(), MoveAbsolute(), MoveFreerun()).

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.DrivePending
member.summary: The SMC sent a command to the drive that will cause the drive to take control of the motion, but the drive has not yet done so.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.DriveAbortPending
member.summary: The SMC sent an abort command to the drive, but the drive has not yet started the abort.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.TrajectoryFiltering
member.summary: Trajectory filtering is enabled for this axis. It was configured with the TrajectoryIirFilter or TrajectoryFirFilter parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.IfovEnabled
member.summary: Infinite Field of View (IFOV) is enabled for this axis. Use the IfovOn() function to enable IFOV. Use the IfovOff() function to disable IFOV.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.NotVirtual
member.summary: A physical drive is associated with this axis. Axes with no drive attached will clear this bit and operate as virtual axes.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.CalibrationEnabled1D
member.summary: The specified 1D calibration file contains a calibration table that corrects this axis.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.CalibrationEnabled2D
member.summary: The specified 2D calibration file contains a calibration table that corrects this axis.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.CammingGearingControl
member.summary: The axis is currently controlled by camming or gearing.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.JoystickControl
member.summary: The axis is currently doing motion under control of the joystick.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.BacklashActive
member.summary: Backlash compensation is enabled for this axis. It was configured with the BacklashDistance parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.GainMappingEnabled
member.summary: A Gain Mapping table was specified for this axis.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.TransformationInput
member.summary: The motion on this axis is the input to a kinematic transformation.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.TransformationOutput
member.summary: The motion on this axis is the output of a kinematic transformation.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.MotionDone
member.summary: Motion on this axis is done, meaning that the velocity command reached zero.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.MotionClamped
member.summary: Motion on this axis is clamped because of a software limit clamp or safe zone.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.GantryAligned
member.summary: This axis is part of a gantry pair and the gantry is correctly aligned. This bit will not be active until the gantry axes have been homed.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.GantryDriveControlRealigning
member.summary: The axis is currently performing gantry realignment motion due to Theta axis misalignment caused by drive motion.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisStatus.ThermoCompEnabled
member.summary: The ThermoComp feature is currently turned on.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.PositionErrorFault
member.summary: The absolute value of the difference between the position command and the position feedback exceeded the threshold specified by the PositionErrorThreshold parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.OverCurrentFault
member.summary: The average motor current exceeded the threshold specified by the AverageCurrentThreshold and AverageCurrentTime parameters.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.CwEndOfTravelLimitFault
member.summary: The axis encountered the clockwise (positive) end-of-travel limit switch.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.CcwEndOfTravelLimitFault
member.summary: The axis encountered the counter-clockwise (negative) end-of-travel limit switch.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.CwSoftwareLimitFault
member.summary: The axis was commanded to move beyond the position specified by the SoftwareLimitHigh parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.CcwSoftwareLimitFault
member.summary: The axis was commanded to move beyond the position specified by the SoftwareLimitLow parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.AmplifierFault
member.summary: The amplifier for this axis exceeded its maximum current rating or experienced an internal error.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.FeedbackInput0Fault
member.summary: The drive detected a problem with the feedback device specified by the FeedbackInput0 parameter. Or, the drive detected an invalid feedback configuration.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.FeedbackInput1Fault
member.summary: The drive detected a problem with the feedback device specified by the FeedbackInput1 parameter. Or, the drive detected an invalid feedback configuration.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.HallSensorFault
member.summary: The drive detected an invalid state (all high or all low) for the Hall-effect sensor inputs on this axis.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.MaxVelocityCommandFault
member.summary: The commanded velocity is more than the velocity command threshold. Before the axis is homed, this threshold is specified by the VelocityCommandThresholdBeforeHome parameter. After the axis is homed, this threshold is specified by the VelocityCommandThreshold parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.EmergencyStopFault
member.summary: The emergency stop sense input was triggered or the controller detected a fault condition in the Safe Torque Off (STO) hardware. The emergency stop sense input is specified with the EmergencyStopFaultInput or SoftwareEmergencyStopInput parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.VelocityErrorFault
member.summary: The absolute value of the difference between the velocity command and the velocity feedback exceeded the threshold specified by the VelocityErrorThreshold parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.CommutationFault
member.summary: The axis attempted commutation, but could not initialize correctly.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.ExternalFault
member.summary: The external fault input, specified by the ExternalFaultAnalogInput, ExternalFaultDigitalInput, or SoftwareExternalFaultInput parameter, was triggered.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.MotorTemperatureFault
member.summary: The motor thermistor input was triggered, which indicates that the motor exceeded its maximum recommended operating temperature.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.AmplifierTemperatureFault
member.summary: The amplifier temperature is not within the operating range or the shunt resistor temperature has exceeded the maximum threshold. This fault occurs at amplifier temperatures less than 0° C or greater than 75° C and shunt resistor temperatures greater than 200° C.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.EncoderFault
member.summary: The encoder fault input on the motor feedback connector was triggered.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.GantryMisalignmentFault
member.summary: The position command or position feedback of the rotary gantry axis exceeded the value specified by the GantryMisalignmentThreshold parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.FeedbackScalingFault
member.summary: The difference between the position feedback and the scaled (adjusted by GainKv) velocity feedback exceeds the threshold specified by the PositionErrorThreshold parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.MarkerSearchFault
member.summary: The distance that the axis moved while searching for the marker exceeded the threshold specified by the MarkerSearchThreshold parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.SafeZoneFault
member.summary: The axis decelerated to a stop because the motion violated a safe zone.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.InPositionTimeoutFault
member.summary: The axis did not achieve in position status in the period specified by the InPositionDisableTimeout parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.VoltageClampFault
member.summary: The commanded voltage output exceeded the value of the PiezoVoltageClampLow or PiezoVoltageClampHigh parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.MotorSupplyFault
member.summary: The amplifier detected that no motor supply voltage is present or that the motor supply voltage is less than the minimum required voltage.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisFault.InternalFault
member.summary: The drive encountered an internal error that caused it to disable. Contact Aerotech Global Technical Support.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.DriveStatus.NoMotorSupply

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.None
member.summary: No bits set.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.SyncFunctionExecuting
member.summary: This enum value is deprecated and will be removed in the next major version of Automation1 software. This enum value has been renamed to SynchronizedFunctionExecuting. Use SynchronizedFunctionExecuting instead of this enum value.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.ProgramAssociated
member.summary: A program is associated with this task.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.ImmediateExecuting
member.summary: An immediate command is executing.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.ReturnMotionExecuting
member.summary: Return motion is executing due to an TaskInterruptMotionOff() function.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.ProgramReset
member.summary: The program is reset.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.PendingAxesStop
member.summary: One or more axes are decelerating. The deceleration could be caused by an abort, a task stop, a task error, a feedhold, an onerror event, or the reverse of motion direction that occurs when retrace activates during rapid (MoveRapid()) or coordinated (MoveLinear(), MoveCw(), MoveCcw()) motion.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.FeedholdActive
member.summary: Bit turns on as soon as motion begins to decelerate due to a feedhold. Bit turns off when motion begins to accelerate back to speed due to a feedhold release. The FeedholdAxesStopped bit of Task Status 1 indicates when deceleration due to a feedhold stops.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.CallbackHoldActive
member.summary: A callback command was issued and is waiting for a front-end application to acknowledge the command.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.CallbackResponding
member.summary: A callback command was issued and is waiting for a front-end application to handle the command.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.SpindleActive
member.summary: The spindle is currently active.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.Retrace
member.summary: Retrace is active.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.InterruptMotionActive
member.summary: Interrupt motion is active.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.JoystickActive
member.summary: The task is executing a JoystickRun() function.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.CornerRounding
member.summary: Corner rounding is enabled.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.JoystickLowSpeedActive
member.summary: The joystick is using the low speed mode.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskStatus0.SynchronizedFunctionExecuting
member.summary: A synchronized function is executing.

member.summary: The task has control restrictions enabled.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerEventArgsCreator
member.summary: A class designed to create event args that require a controller anywhere without directly passing a instance throughout the .NET API. This helps limit the APIs in .NET so they can't use the instance to do anything from anywhere. To create a controller exception call one of the many "create" methods that are exposed through this class. The reference will be passed to the contructor of the event args for you.
member.remarks: This object should only be constructed in one place: the constructor on because that is the only time a object is available to pass to this classes constructor. No one else in the .NET API will ever use a object directly and no one else should construct this object.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerEventArgsCreator.#ctor(Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Controller)
member.summary: Constructs a new object. Do not call this constructor directly unless you are in the constructor of the class. Calling this constructor manually is an indication that something is wrong (e.g. you are passing a reference to directly to some API in the .NET API and you shouldn't be).
member.param: The parent controller that all event args will be constructed with.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisSet
member.summary: Represents the set of axes defined on a controller, with ways to validate axis name and axis index and then convert between them. See remarks.
member.remarks:
The purpose behind this internal class is to be a centralized collection for the axes on a controller. APIs should be written to use a set instead of directly using a object. This decouples the APIs from the controller making them cleaner and unit testable (a set can easily be mocked). If an API needs to know about axes, it should consume a . This class should always be used instead of a class storing axes itself.
The class requires an to resolve its user input into an actual, valid controller axis.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisSet.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: Constructor, taking in an sequence of axis names that are at the array index that matches their axis index.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisSet.Axes
member.summary: Gets the list of axis names, which are at the array index that matches their axis index.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisSet.GetMaxAxisCount
member.summary: Returns the maximum number of axes supported on the controller.
member.remarks: This doesn't return the theoretical maximum number of axes, this returns the specific maximum on the controller right now.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisSet.ValidateAxisName(System.String)
member.summary: Validates an axis name, throwing an exception if validation fails because it is not a correct axis on the controller.
member.remarks: This doesn't check whether a theoretical axis name is valid, this checks whether a specific axis name is valid and exists on the controller right now.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisSet.ValidateAxisIndex(System.Int32)
member.summary: Validates an axis index, throwing an exception if validation fails because it is not a correct axis on the controller.
member.remarks: This doesn't check whether a theoretical axis index is valid, this checks whether a specific axis index is valid and exists on the controller right now.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisSet.GetAxisIndexFromAxisName(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the corresponding axis index from the controller based on an axis name.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisSet.GetAxisNameFromAxisIndex(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the corresponding axis name from the controller based on an axis index.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisSet.GetAxisIndicesFromAxisNames(System.String[])
member.summary: Returns a list of axis indices from the controller based on the list of axis names.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AxisSet.GetAxisNamesFromAxisIndices(System.Int32[])
member.summary: Returns a list axis names from the controller based on the list of axis indices.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Constants
member.summary: Various internal constants.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Constants.MaxTheoreticalAxisCount
member.summary: Gets the theoretical maximum number of supported axes for any type of Automation1 controller. This is always 32, no Automation1 controller type supports more than 32 axes. An axis index must be below this number, i.e.: [0, ). There may be fewer supported axes depending on the type of controller (i.e. drive-based), but never more than this. You probably shouldn't use this property and instead get the actual number of supported axes from the controller.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Constants.MaxTheoreticalTaskCount
member.summary: Gets the theoretical maximum number of supported tasks for any type of Automation1 controller. This is always 32, no Automation1 controller type supports more than 32 tasks. A task index must be below this number, i.e.: [0, ). There may be fewer supported tasks depending on the type of controller (i.e. drive-based), but never more than this. You probably shouldn't use this property and instead get the actual number of supported tasks from the controller.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Constants.MaxPcBasedAxisCount
member.summary: Gets the maximum number of supported axes for a PC-Based controller. You probably shouldn't use this property and instead get the actual number of supported axes from the controller.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Constants.MaxPcBasedTaskCount
member.summary: Gets the maximum number of supported tasks for a PC-Based controller. You probably shouldn't use this property and instead get the actual number of supported axes from the controller.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Constants.MaxDriveBasedAxisCount
member.summary: Gets the maximum number of supported axes for a Drive-Based controller. You probably shouldn't use this property and instead get the actual number of supported axes from the controller.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Constants.MaxDriveBasedTaskCount
member.summary: Gets the maximum number of supported axes for a Drive-Based controller. You probably shouldn't use this property and instead get the actual number of supported axes from the controller.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Constants.ReservedTaskIndex
member.summary: Gets the task index for the reserved task which is not usable be users. This may be null. If this is null, it does not mean there is no reserved task, instead it means this instance of the .NET API is authorized to use the reserved task. Automation1 Studio will set this to null to remove the reserved task restriction because the 0th task is reserved for the Studio itself.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Constants.DefaultTaskIndex
member.summary: Gets the index of the default task to be used when a task is not specified by the user.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerContext
member.summary: An enumeration for use when you need to keep track of items that could belong to axis, task, or system contexts. WARNING: Do _not_ use these values outside of this library (i.e. do not pass them into a request to the controller or use them in other libraries).
member.remarks: The values are deliberately huge to make sure they can't be accidentally used in a controller request.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.McdManager
member.summary: A class to upload and download Machine Controller Definitions to and from the Automation1 controller.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.McdManager.#ctor(Aerotech.Automation1.Communication.Requester)
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.McdManager.DownloadMcdToFile(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Downloads a machine controller definition (.mcd) file from the controller and saves it to the specified MDK file path. A downloaded machine controller definition file can contain both controller configuration and controller files. This file can be uploaded to any controller to copy this controller's configuration and files. When using this method, at least one of the parameters shouldIncludeControllerFiles and shouldIncludeConfiguration must be true.
member.param: The MDK file path to save the downloaded machine controller definition (.mcd) file at.
Whether or not controller files should be included in the download.
Whether or not controller configuration should be included in the download.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.McdManager.UploadMcdToController(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Uploads the machine controller definition (.mcd) file to the controller from the specified MDK file path. When using this method, at least one of the parameters shouldIncludeControllerFiles and shouldIncludeConfiguration must be true. Before calling this method, make sure there is nothing important on the controller that could be overwritten or erased. Consider downloading a machine controller definition file as a backup before uploading a different machine controller definition that may overwrite data.
member.param: The MDK file path of the machine controller definition to upload.
Whether or not controller files should be included in the upload.
Whether or not controller configuration should be included in the upload.
Whether or not the controller should be erased before uploading the new files.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.RequestHelper.GetControllerUnitsParameters(Aerotech.Automation1.Communication.Requester,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns all controller parameters required to convert the units of a status item or data signal as well as the parameters required to determine the name of a status item' or data signal's units.
member.remarks: This method gets both double parameters and string parameters from the controller. Be careful when you use it; getting string parameters from the controller is not particularly performant and thus the parameter was added. Set that parameter to false to avoid getting string parameters. Be aware that you won't be able to determine the name of the status item's or data signal's units if you do so.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskSet
member.summary: Represents the set of tasks defined on a controller, with ways to validate task name and task index and then convert between them. See remarks.
member.remarks:
The purpose behind this internal class is to be a centralized collection for the tasks on a controller. APIs should be written to use a set instead of directly using a object. This decouples the APIs from the controller making them cleaner and unit testable (a set can easily be mocked). If an API needs to know about tasks, it should consume a . This class should always be used instead of a class storing tasks itself.
The class requires a to resolve its user input into an actual, valid controller task.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskSet.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: Constructor, taking in an sequence of task names that are at the array index that matches their task index.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskSet.Tasks
member.summary: Gets the list of task names, which are at the array index that matches their task index.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskSet.GetMaxTaskCount
member.summary: Returns the maximum number of tasks supported on the controller.
member.remarks: This doesn't return the theoretical maximum number of tasks, this returns the specific maximum on the controller right now.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskSet.ValidateTaskName(System.String)
member.summary: Validates a task name, throwing an exception if validation fails because it is not a correct task on the controller.
member.remarks: This doesn't check whether a theoretical task name is valid, this checks whether a specific task name is valid and exists on the controller right now.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.TaskSet.ValidateTaskIndex(System.Int32)
member.summary: Validates a task index, throwing an exception if validation fails because it is not a correct task on the controller.
member.remarks: This doesn't check whether a theoretical task index is valid, this checks whether a specific task index is valid and exists on the controller right now.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus
member.summary: A class that continuously and automatically retrieves status from the Automation1 controller. To retrieve status, subscribe to the event. To pause automatic status, call . The event will not be raised while the controller is stopped or resetting even if automatic status is not paused. It will continue to be raised once the controller is running again. If an exception occurs while retrieving status, status retrieval is paused. To see the exception that caused status retrieval to pause, subscribe to the event. To start retrieving status again, call . Automatically retrieving status has a default status item configuration. To see this default status item configuration, call . To change the status item configuration, call .

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.#ctor(Aerotech.Automation1.Communication.Requester,Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerEventArgsCreator,Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.IStatus,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: E:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.NewStatusRetrieved
member.summary: Raised when new status is retrieved from the Automation1 controller. This event will not be raised while the controller is stopped or resetting even if automatic status is not paused. It will continue to be raised once the controller is running again. By default this event will retrieve common status items for every axis and task (see ), but you can change the status item configuration by calling . To prevent this event from being raised, call .

member.name: E:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.ExceptionOccurred
member.summary: Raised when an exception occurs while trying to retrieve status from the Automation1 controller. See the property to see the exception that occurred. If this event is raised, status retrieval has been paused. To continue retrieving status call .

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.IsPaused
member.summary: Gets whether or not automatic status retrieval is paused. If automatic status retrieval is paused, the will not be raised.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.Period
member.summary: Get or set the period (milliseconds) in which status should be retrieved from the Automation1 controller. This determines how often is raised.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.GetDefaultConfiguration
member.summary: Gets the default status item configuration which is used if you don't change the configuration via . This default status item configuration will retrieve common status items for every axis and task.
member.remarks:
The default status item configuration configures the following axis status items for each axis:
The default status item configuration configures the follow task status item for each task:
member.returns: The default status item configuration.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.SetConfiguration(Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.StatusItemConfiguration)
member.summary: Sets the status item configuration that will be used to retrieve status from the Automation1 controller. Changing the configuration will change the results that are retrieved when the event is raised.
member.param: What status items to retrieve from the Automation1 controller.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.GetConfiguration
member.summary: Gets the status item configuration that is being used to retrieve status from the Automation1 controller. To change the status item configuration being used, see .
member.returns: What status items are being retrieved from the Automation1 controller.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.Resume
member.summary: Resumes retrieving status from the Automation1 controller.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.Pause
member.summary: Pauses retrieving status from the Automation1 controller until is called. Pausing status retrieval prevents from being raised.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.Shutdown
member.summary: Shutdown this instance of . Once automatic status has been shutdown it cannot be resumed. An new object must be created.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.createDefaultStatusItemConfiguration(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates the default status item configuration that is used to automatically retrieve status items from the Automation1 controller.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.startPollingThread
member.summary: Start the thread that polls for status. This thread is a long running thread that lives for the life time of the object.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AutomaticStatus.pollingWork(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: The method containing the polling work that retrieves status from the controller.
member.param: The cancellation token that causes the thread to die.

member.summary: An interface to retrieve status from an Automation1 controller.

member.summary: Returns retrieved results for requested status items from the Automation1 controller.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Status
member.summary: A class to retrieve status from an Automation1 controller. Access an instance of this class through a object instance.
member.remarks: Retrieved status represents a moment in time. Status is different from data collection in that status is not real-time and it is not time-series data. Status also has variable latency when retrieving.

member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Status.Automatic
member.summary: Gets a way to continuously and automatically retrieve status from the Automation1 controller. To automatically retrieve status, subscribe to the event.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Status.GetStatusItems(Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.StatusItemConfiguration)
member.summary: Returns retrieved results for requested status items from the Automation1 controller.
member.remarks: The retrieved status represents a moment in time. Retrieving status is not real-time and there is variable latency when calling this method. Construct a and add status items to it to retrieve them from the Automation1 controller.
member.param: What status items to retrieve from the Automation1 controller.
member.returns: Returns a that contains the results for the retrieved status items.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.Status.ConvertUnits(Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerUnits,Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.StatusItemResults)
member.summary: Converts the data within a object to use different units. How the data in a object is converted is dependent on the Automation1 controller's configured parameters. See for how each unit type is dependent on configured parameters. See to see how to change configured parameters on the Automation1 controller.
member.param: The units to convert to.
The results to convert.
member.returns: Returns a new object which contains data that has been converted to use a different set of units.

member.summary: Creates a request for the specified to be sent to the controller.
Creates a based on the response from the controller.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.AppMessageBox
member.summary: A class to register and unregister to the app message box that could be raised by an AeroScript program on a controller task on an Automation1 controller. member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskBreakpoints
member.summary:
A class to manage breakpoints for AeroScript programs and libraries on a task on an Automation1 controller. Breakpoints are added to AeroScript source files in order to debug running programs and libraries.
Breakpoints added to AeroScript programs or static libraries are unique per task. Executing the same AeroScript program or static library on different tasks will have different breakpoints. To clear all breakpoints from a program or static library for this task use the method.
Breakpoints added to AeroScript dynamic libraries are the same across all tasks. Executing two different programs that import the same dynamic library will have the same dynamic library breakpoints but different program breakpoints. To clear all breakpoints from a dynamic library for all tasks use the method.
Access an instance of this class through a logged in object and then through the property.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskBreakpoints.AddTo(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Adds a breakpoint to an AeroScript source file at the specified line number. When AeroScript execution reaches the specified line number in the specified source file, the running program will pause.
Breakpoints added to programs or static libraries are unique per task. Breakpoints added to dynamic libraries are the same across all tasks.
member.param: The AeroScript source file to add a breakpoint to.
The AeroScript source line number to add a breakpoint to.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskBreakpoints.RemoveFrom(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes a breakpoint from an AeroScript source file at the specified line number.
member.param: The AeroScript source file to remove a breakpoint from.
The AeroScript source line number to remove a breakpoint from.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskBreakpoints.GetAll(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the list of all breakpoints added to an AeroScript source file.
member.param: The AeroScript source file to get the breakpoints for.
member.returns: The list of AeroScript source line numbers that have breakpoints for the AeroScript source file on the specified task.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskBreakpoints.ClearAll(System.String)
member.summary: Removes all breakpoints from an AeroScript source file.
member.param: The AeroScript source file to remove all breakpoints from.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskCallback
member.summary: A class to register and unregister callbacks that could be raised by an AeroScript program on a controller task on an Automation1 controller. Callbacks can only be registered a single time. To change the registration of the callback, unregister the callback first. To do that, see . To uniquely identify a callback, check against the property and the property which can be accessed through the object that is provided to your registered handler. Access an instance of this class through a object instance and then through the property.

member.summary: Registers a callback that could be raised by an AeroScript program on this task. Callbacks can only be registered to a single time. To change the registration of the callback, unregister it first. To do that, see .
member.param: A number that is uniquely identifies the callback per task. This must have a value between 0 and 65535.
The code to execute when the callback is raised by an AeroScript program on this task.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskDebug
member.summary: A class to debug running AeroScript programs and libraries on a task on an Automation1 controller (manage breakpoints, step into, step over, etc.). Access an instance of this class through a object instance and then through the property.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskDebug.#ctor(System.Int32,Aerotech.Automation1.Communication.Requester)
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskDebug.Breakpoints
member.summary:
Gets a way to manage breakpoints for AeroScript programs and libraries on this task. Breakpoints are added to AeroScript source files in order to debug running programs and libraries. When AeroScript execution encounters a breakpoint in a source file, the running program will pause.
Breakpoints added to AeroScript programs or static libraries are unique per task. Executing the same AeroScript program or static library on different tasks will have different breakpoints.
Breakpoints added to AeroScript dynamic libraries are the same across all tasks. Executing two different programs that import the same dynamic library will have the same dynamic library breakpoints but different program breakpoints.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskDebug.StepInto
member.summary: Performs a single line of program execution on the currently paused AeroScript program on this task, stepping into any function calls. This method can only be called if there is an AeroScript program loaded on this task and it is paused.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskDebug.StepOver
member.summary: Performs a single line of program execution on the currently paused AeroScript program on this task, stepping over any function calls. This method can only be called if there is an AeroScript program loaded on this task and it is paused.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskDebug.StepOut
member.summary: Performs execution on the currently paused AeroScript program on this task, stepping out of the current function call. This method can only be called if there is an AeroScript program loaded on this task and it is paused.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskMotionControl
member.summary: A class to control the motion on a task on an Automation1 controller (enable/disable retrace mode, enable/disable feedhold state, set MFO, etc.). Access an instance of this class through a object instance and then through the property.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskMotionControl.#ctor(System.Int32,Aerotech.Automation1.Communication.Requester)
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskMotionControl.RetraceOn
member.summary: Enables retrace mode for the running AeroScript program on this task. When you enable retrace mode, the controller executes motion and motion-synchronized functions in reverse order, through functions that were previously executed. The controller keeps a history of moves and synchronized commands that it can use for retrace.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskMotionControl.RetraceOff
member.summary: Disables retrace mode for the running AeroScript program on this task. When you disable retrace mode, the controller executes forward through the retrace history until it moves to the program line where retrace was activated.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskMotionControl.FeedholdOn
member.summary: Enables the feedhold state for the AeroScript program on this task. Enabling the feedhold state causes motion on this task to immediately decelerate and it stops the execution of programs and immediate commands on this task.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskMotionControl.FeedholdOff
member.summary: Disables the feedhold state on this task. Disabling the feedhold state causes motion on this task to accelerate back up to the speed it was before feedhold was enabled. Also, the execution of programs and immediate commands on this task continues from where it left off once the feedhold state is disabled.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskMotionControl.SetMfo(System.Double)
member.summary: Sets the MFO (manual feedrate override) percentage for this task which scales the programmed feedrate for all coordinated motion and motion generated by the MoveRapid() AeroScript function. The MFO percentage multiplied by the programmed feedrate determines the true feedrate of the move. To get the current value of MFO for this task, use Status () to retrieve TaskStatusItem.Mfo.
member.param: The MFO percentage to set.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl
member.summary: A class to control AeroScript programs on a task on an Automation1 controller (load, start, stop, etc.). Access an instance of this class through a object instance and then through the property.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl.#ctor(System.Int32,Aerotech.Automation1.Communication.Requester,Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ICompiler)
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl.Run(System.String)
member.summary: Runs the specified controller file as an AeroScript program on this task. If the controller file is an AeroScript source file then the file will first be compiled. Running a program will automatically load the program and begin program execution on this task. This method waits for the program to load and start but it does not wait for the program to complete.
member.remarks: The AeroScript program must exist as a controller file on the Automation1 controller (see ). AeroScript libraries cannot be run on a task, the controller file must be an AeroScript program (either a source program file or a compiled program file).
member.param: The controller file that is the AeroScript program to run on this task (source program file or compiled program file).

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl.Run(Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.CompiledAeroScript)
member.summary: Runs a compiled AeroScript program on this task. Running a program will automatically load the program and begin program execution on this task. This method waits for the program to load and start but it does not wait for the program to complete. To compile an AeroScript program source file, see .
member.remarks: AeroScript libraries cannot be run on a task, must be a compiled AeroScript program.
member.param: The compiled AeroScript program to run on this task.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl.Load(System.String)
member.summary: Loads the specified controller file as an AeroScript program on this task. If the controller file is an AeroScript source file then the file will first be compiled. Loading does not start the program, you must call to begin program execution on this task.
member.remarks: The AeroScript program must exist as a controller file on the Automation1 controller (see ). AeroScript libraries cannot be loaded on a task, the controller file must be an AeroScript program (either a source program file or a compiled program file).
member.param: The controller file that is the AeroScript program to load on this task (source program file or compiled program file).

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl.Load(Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.CompiledAeroScript)
member.summary: Loads a compiled AeroScript program on this task. Loading does not start the program, you must call to begin program execution on this task. To compile a program see .
member.remarks: AeroScript libraries cannot be loaded on a task, must be a compiled AeroScript program.
member.param: The compiled AeroScript program to load on this task.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl.Start
member.summary: Starts or resumes the currently loaded AeroScript program on this task, beginning program execution. A program must be loaded on to this task to be able to start or resume it. To load a program, call . This method waits for the loaded program to start but it does not wait for the program to complete.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl.Stop
member.summary: Stops the currently running AeroScript program on this task, terminating program execution. This method will wait indefinitely for the loaded program to stop. After a program is stopped it is unloaded from the task. This method will also end a command queue running on this task.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl.Stop(System.Int32)
member.summary: Stops the currently running AeroScript program on this task, terminating program execution. This method will wait for the specified amount of time for the loaded program to stop. After a program is stopped it is unloaded from the task. This method will also end a command queue running on this task.
member.param: The number of milliseconds to wait to stop the program on this task. If the program takes longer than this amount of time to stop, an exception is thrown.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl.Pause
member.summary: Pauses the currently running AeroScript program on this task, suspending program execution. This method will wait indefinitely for the loaded program to pause. To resume program execution, call .

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskProgramControl.Pause(System.Int32)
member.summary: Pauses the currently running AeroScript program on this task, suspending program execution. This method will wait for the specified amount of time for the loaded program to pause. To resume program execution, call .
member.param: The number of milliseconds to wait to pause the program on this task. If the program takes longer than this amount of time to pause, an exception is thrown.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerDistanceUnit
member.summary: Represents distance units that the Automation1 controller understands. How the Automation1 controller understands secondary units can be modified by updating the SecondaryUnitsScaleFactor and SecondaryUnitsName system parameters. How the Automation1 controller understands counts can be modified by updating the CountsPerUnit axis parameter.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerDistanceUnit.Primary
member.summary: Represents the default units that the Automation1 controller uses. All values from data collection and status are provided as primary units.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerDistanceUnit.Secondary
member.summary: Represents an additional set of units that the Automation1 controller understands. To change how the Automation1 understands secondary units, modify the SecondaryUnitsScaleFactor and SecondaryUnitsName system parameters.

member.name: F:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerDistanceUnit.Counts
member.summary: Represents counts as understood by the Automation1 controller. To change how the Automation1 understands counts, modify the CountsPerUnit axis parameter.

member.summary: Represents time units that an Automation1 controller understands.

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerGlobalVariables
member.summary: A class to get and set controller global variables on an Automation1 controller. The controller has a set of global variable arrays for each of the three data types: AeroScript integer values ($iglobal in AeroScript), AeroScript real values ($rglobal in AeroScript), and AeroScript string values ($sglobal in AeroScript). These controller global variables are accessible from every task on the controller and from every API. These variables can be used to communicate data between tasks or between the controller and a custom application. The controller parameters , , and specify the total number of elements in each respective global variable array. Access an instance of this class through a object instance.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerGlobalVariables.#ctor(Aerotech.Automation1.Communication.Requester)
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: M:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerGlobalVariables.GetInteger(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the value of an integer controller global variable ($iglobal in AeroScript), at the specified global integer array index. The value is returned as an AeroScript integer value (which is a 64-bit long integer).

member.name: T:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerTaskVariables
member.summary: A class to get and set task variables on a task on an Automation1 controller. Each task on the controller has a set of task variable arrays for each of the three data types: AeroScript integer values ($itask in AeroScript), AeroScript real values ($rtask in AeroScript), and AeroScript string values ($stask in AeroScript). These task variables are accessible from every API and can be used to communicate data between iterations of a program running on the same task. They are not accessible from other tasks. The controller parameters , , and specify the total number of elements in each respective task variable array. Access an instance of this class through a object instance and then through the property.

member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerVariables.Global
member.summary: Gets a way to get and set controller global variables on the Automation1 controller. The controller has a set of global variable arrays for each of the three data types: AeroScript integer values ($iglobal in AeroScript), AeroScript real values ($rglobal in AeroScript), and AeroScript string values ($sglobal in AeroScript). These controller global variables are accessible from every task on the controller and from every API. These variables can be used to communicate data between tasks or between the controller and a custom application. The controller parameters , , and specify the total number of elements in each respective global variable array.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerVariables.IndustrialEthernet
member.summary: Gets a way to get and set Industrial Ethernet variables on the Automation1 controller.

member.name: P:Aerotech.Automation1.DotNet.ControllerVariables.Task
member.summary: Gets a way to get and set task variables on a task on the Automation1 controller. Each task on the controller has a set of task variable arrays for each of the three data types: AeroScript integer values ($itask in AeroScript), AeroScript real values ($rtask in AeroScript), and AeroScript string values ($stask in AeroScript). These task variables are accessible from every API and can be used to communicate data between iterations of a program running on the same task. They are not accessible from other tasks. The controller parameters , , and specify the total number of elements in each respective task variable array.
